phrases.intent_en: Ask where the Impressionist galleries are
phrases.text_local: Où se trouvent les salles des impressionnistes ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV lay sal dayz ehm-preh-syoh-NEEST
phrases.cultural_note: The Impressionist masterpieces are on the top floor (niveau 5). Head straight up to see Monet, Renoir, Degas, and more with beautiful natural light.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the Van Gogh paintings are
phrases.text_local: Où sont les tableaux de Van Gogh ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo sohn lay tah-BLOH duh vahn GOGH
phrases.cultural_note: The French pronounce Van Gogh closer to 'vahn GOGH' (with a soft French 'g'). His self-portraits and Starry Night Over the Rhône are highlights.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the famous clock windows
phrases.text_local: Peut-on voir les horloges depuis l'intérieur ?
phrases.pronunciation: puh-TOHN vwahr layz or-LOHZH duh-PWEE lahn-tay-RYUHR
phrases.cultural_note: The giant clock face on the 5th floor offers a stunning view of Montmartre through the glass. It's one of the most photographed spots in the museum.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the building's history as a train station
phrases.cultural_note: The Musée d'Orsay was the Gare d'Orsay train station, built for the 1900 World's Fair. It was converted into a museum in 1986.

phrases.text_local: Avez-vous des Nymphéas de Monet ici ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah-VAY voo day nehm-fay-AH duh moh-NEH ee-SEE
phrases.cultural_note: The Orsay has some Monet water lilies, but the most famous large panels are at the Musée de l'Orangerie in the Tuileries Garden — just a short walk away.
